behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue interaction plus signature verification result propagation. A pure Unit test of only HMAC comparison would miss accidental enqueue on reject. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about header/body validation and forwarding control, with no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real routing/middleware and a test queue adapter that records writes. Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` and assert `401` plus zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and queue persistence). Unit is too low because the bug appears at handoff/order boundary. Rejected System: behavior text requires write-before-ack ordering, not real network/socket behavior; can be caught with in-process server plus instrumented queue.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP request handling with the real queue module (or test persistence backend) and timestamps/hooks for `enqueue committed` and `response sent`. Assert enqueue commit occurs before `200` response is finalized.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Need to verify decoupling between request path and downstream processor module. Unit-only logic tests can miss accidental `await` on processor promise. Rejected System: behavior text is about async control flow, not real network or subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream processor in-process. Make processor block (e.g., unresolved promise) after enqueue starts. Assert HTTP response is returned promptly while processor remains unfinished.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Wall-clock latency under concurrency is sensitive to real event-loop scheduling, contention, and runtime behavior; in-process fakes or mocked timing can hide failures. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output quality or multi-agent chain is involved per behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process with realistic concurrency (multiple in-flight requests plus intentionally slow downstream workers on earlier events). Measure end-to-end response times over real HTTP and assert each new webhook response is <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at module/state boundary (handler + persistent queue/dedup store). Unit is too low if it tests only ID-check helper without persistence interaction. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/OS primitives; local persistence wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Send two requests with same event ID through in-process handler against the real dedup/persistence layer. Assert first write succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is error translation between queue module and HTTP layer. Unit testing only header formatting misses contract mapping bugs. Rejected System: behavior text explicitly says 'queue reports it is full' (deterministic module signal), so real network/subprocess infra is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler with queue component configured/stubbed to return `queue_full`. Assert HTTP `503` and presence/value format of `Retry-After`, and no success ack path.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue implementation with its persistence backend/config interaction; a unit mock of storage could hide enforcement bugs. Rejected System: behavior text is about configured capacity enforcement in the queue itself, which is typically catchable with local DB/filesystem integration without real network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against a local test backend with capacity N. Enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection/error and unchanged stored count.
